title: Parallax pre-freeze review budget state
description: Machine-written audit state for bounded pre-freeze verifier rounds. Lives at .parallax/<slug>/reviews/pre-freeze-state.json and is written only by scripts/pre-freeze-budget.py. v0.37.3 F3: the required 'closure' object mechanically separates the two trust levels a bare all_resolved boolean used to collapse — an INDEPENDENT verifier pass ('independent-pass', written by record() only when that round's schema-valid verdict is pass) versus any orchestrator/human self-attestation, which this schema deliberately cannot represent: the status enum has no self-attested/orchestrator/human-approved member, and additionalProperties:false rejects any bolted-on all_resolved-style field.

mode.description: v0.37.5 F3 NEW-MODE (5.1): the invocation mode this pre-freeze review belongs to, pinned at state init from the run's own flags and immutable for the life of the state. This makes the freeze-gate MODE BINDING checkable from the artifact: an --autonomous run can no longer freeze through the interactive human-OK branch by being relabeled at the console — freeze-check reads mode.autonomous from HERE (cross-checked against the caller's --mode on every command) and fails closed unless closure.status is independent-pass. In autonomous mode `grants` must stay empty (a human round-grant is an interactive affordance; autonomous parks instead) — validate_state_semantics enforces it.

closure.description: Freeze-sufficiency state of the pre-freeze review. 'open' = not independently closed (the only state a concerns round, a budget cap, a human grant, or any orchestrator note can produce); 'independent-pass' = the last recorded round's own schema-valid verdict was pass. Written exclusively by scripts/pre-freeze-budget.py record; the script additionally cross-checks on every read that an independent-pass closure names the LAST round, that round's verdict is pass, and the artifact matches — a hand-edited closure fails the gate rather than certifying it.
